frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) priming
frameworks.one_line: cathodal priming before anodal stimulation to modulate motor cortex plasticity

frameworks.name: motor skill learning and retention
frameworks.one_line: behavioural framework assessing speed-accuracy trade-off across training and retention

core_question: Do multiple consecutive sessions of cathodal-then-anodal tDCS over sensorimotor cortex further enhance plateau-stage visuomotor learning and retention beyond anodal or sham stimulation?
key_assumptions: cathodal priming homeostatically biases subsequent anodal effects on M1 plasticity
plateau-stage visuomotor learning can still be modulated by stimulation
speed-accuracy trade-off (index of performance) indexes motor learning
manipulations: stimulation condition (c-a-tDCS vs a-tDCS vs sham) between groups
multiple consecutive training days with online and offline tDCS
circular-tracing task across baseline, training, and retention blocks
key_findings: c-a-tDCS significantly increased index of performance above baseline across 3 training days
performance gains were maintained across 2 retention days
improvements under c-a-tDCS did not significantly differ from sham
interpretations: multiple-session cathodal priming yields within-group learning gains but no reliable advantage over sham for already-learned plateau task
